faqs.question: Why is Pooler Parkway so dangerous near Godley Station?
faqs.answer: Pooler Parkway handles massive traffic volumes from Godley Station's residential population, Tanger Outlets shoppers, and commuters heading to I-16 and I-95. faqs.question: Can I file a claim for a truck accident near Godley Station?
faqs.answer: Yes. Trucking companies can be held liable for accidents caused by their drivers under Georgia law. Pooler's proximity to the Port of Savannah and I-16/I-95 means heavy truck traffic throughout the area. Our attorneys understand federal trucking regulations and can pursue claims against trucking companies.

faqs.question: How long do I have to file an injury claim in Georgia?
faqs.answer: Georgia has a 2-year statute of limitations for personal injury claims (O.C.G.A. § 9-3-33). This is shorter than many states, so contacting an attorney promptly is critical.

faqs.question: Can I file a claim for a construction zone accident near the Hyundai Metaplant?
faqs.answer: Yes. Construction companies, contractors, and developers must maintain safe conditions for workers and the public. If a construction zone hazard — debris, lane shifts, poor signage, equipment on the road — caused your accident, the responsible parties may be liable.

faqs.question: What if an 18-wheeler causes my accident on I-16?
faqs.answer: Trucking companies and their insurers can be held liable for accidents caused by their drivers. Federal Motor Carrier Safety Regulations impose strict requirements on truck maintenance, driver hours, and load securement. Violations of these regulations can strengthen your case.

faqs.question: Is Georgia's statute of limitations different from South Carolina?
faqs.answer: Yes. Georgia has a 2-year statute of limitations (O.C.G.A. § 9-3-33), compared to South Carolina's 3 years. This shorter deadline makes prompt legal action critical for West Pooler accident victims.

faqs.question: Can I sue Oglethorpe Mall if I'm injured on their property?
faqs.answer: Yes. Mall owners must maintain safe conditions for visitors. If poor maintenance, inadequate lighting, wet floors, or parking lot hazards caused your injury, the property owner or management company may be liable under Georgia premises liability law.

faqs.question: What is Georgia's comparative fault rule?
faqs.answer: Georgia follows a modified comparative fault rule — you can recover damages as long as you are less than 50% at fault (O.C.G.A. § 51-12-33). Your recovery is reduced by your percentage of fault.

Bull Street through the center of the district handles both local and tourist traffic alongside trolley tours, horse-drawn carriages, and heavy pedestrian crossings at each square. neighborhoods.content_hook:
Savannah's Historic District is one of the largest National Historic Landmark Districts in the country, featuring 22 park-like squares in a grid pattern that was revolutionary in the 18th century but creates complex traffic challenges in the 21st. The district draws millions of tourists annually, mixing heavy pedestrian, trolley, carriage, and vehicle traffic through narrow streets.
Roden Law's Savannah office represents Historic District residents and visitors injured in car accidents around the squares, trolley and carriage incidents, pedestrian collisions on Bull Street and Bay Street, bicycle crashes, and premises liability at hotels, restaurants, and tourist attractions.
Injured in Savannah's Historic District? Call (912) 303-5850 for a free case review.

faqs.question: Are pedestrian accidents common in Savannah's Historic District?
faqs.answer: Yes. The combination of millions of annual tourists, narrow streets, 22 square intersections, trolley tours, and horse-drawn carriages creates one of the most complex pedestrian environments in the Southeast. Bull Street and Bay Street are particular hotspots for vehicle-pedestrian conflicts.

faqs.question: Can I sue a trolley tour company if their vehicle injures me?
faqs.answer: Yes. Trolley tour operators have a duty to operate safely and maintain their vehicles. If operator negligence, mechanical failure, or unsafe operation caused your injury, the tour company may be liable. Georgia law holds commercial operators to a high standard of care.

faqs.question: Who is liable for a trip-and-fall on an uneven brick sidewalk in the Historic District?
faqs.answer: The City of Savannah or the adjacent property owner may be liable, depending on who is responsible for sidewalk maintenance. Savannah's historic brick and stone surfaces create frequent trip hazards. Georgia's 2-year statute of limitations applies, so contact an attorney promptly.

neighborhoods.content_hook:
River Street is Savannah's most iconic tourist destination, a cobblestone-lined waterfront promenade along the Savannah River featuring restaurants, bars, shops, and galleries in converted cotton warehouses. While the historic cobblestones and steep ramps are part of River Street's charm, they also create one of the most hazardous pedestrian environments in Savannah.
Roden Law's Savannah office handles personal injury claims on River Street, including slip-and-fall injuries on cobblestones and ramps, premises liability at bars and restaurants, alcohol-related incidents, and pedestrian accidents involving vehicles and delivery trucks on the narrow roadway.

faqs.question: Can I sue if I slip and fall on River Street's cobblestones?
faqs.answer: Potentially yes. While the cobblestones are a historic feature, the City of Savannah and adjacent property owners have a duty to maintain reasonably safe conditions. If unusually hazardous conditions — such as excessive moisture, missing stones, or inadequate warnings — caused your fall, liability may exist.

faqs.question: Can I file a dram shop claim against a River Street bar?
faqs.answer: Georgia's dram shop law (O.C.G.A. § 51-1-40) allows claims against establishments that serve alcohol to noticeably intoxicated persons who then cause injury to others. If a River Street bar overserved the person who injured you, the bar may share liability.

faqs.question: What if I'm hit by a delivery truck on River Street?
faqs.answer: Commercial vehicles delivering to River Street businesses must operate safely in a heavy-pedestrian environment. The trucking or delivery company and their driver may be liable if their negligence caused your injury. An attorney can determine all responsible parties.

This limited-access corridor handles traffic volumes far beyond its design capacity, creating dangerous congestion, rear-end collisions, and bridge-related accidents.

faqs.question: Can I file a claim for a boating accident near Wilmington Island?
faqs.answer: Yes. Boating accidents in the Bull River, Lazaretto Creek, and surrounding waterways fall under maritime and Georgia watercraft law. The at-fault boat operator, marina, or commercial operator may be liable. Roden Law handles boating accident cases throughout Chatham County.

faqs.question: What if flooding on Johnny Mercer Boulevard causes my accident?
faqs.answer: If the government entity responsible for road maintenance failed to address known flooding hazards — such as inadequate drainage, missing flood warnings, or poor road design — they may be liable under the Georgia Tort Claims Act. An attorney can investigate the circumstances.

neighborhoods.name: Port Wentworth Town Center
neighborhoods.h1: Personal Injury Lawyer Serving Port Wentworth, GA
neighborhoods.meta_title: Port Wentworth Personal Injury Lawyer | Roden Law | Free Consult
neighborhoods.meta_description: Injured in Port Wentworth? Roden Law handles truck accidents on I-95/I-16, Hwy 21 crashes, and port corridor injuries. Free consultation.
neighborhoods.roads: Highway 21 through Port Wentworth carries heavy truck traffic from the Port of Savannah's Garden City Terminal, mixing 18-wheelers with residential commuter traffic at dangerous speed differentials. The I-95 and I-16 interchange area near Port Wentworth generates some of the region's most dangerous high-speed interstate collisions. Bourne Avenue and Crossgate Drive handle local traffic in areas increasingly surrounded by warehouse and logistics development. Port-related truck traffic on Highway 307 and surrounding roads creates constant conflicts with passenger vehicles.
neighborhoods.hospitals:
Memorial Health University Medical Center — 4700 Waters Ave, Savannah, GA 31404 — (912) 350-8000
St. Joseph's Hospital — 11705 Mercy Blvd, Savannah, GA — (912) 819-4100
neighborhoods.landmarks: Port of Savannah (adjacent), I-95/I-16 interchange area, Highway 21 commercial corridor, Port Wentworth City Hall, industrial and logistics corridor
neighborhoods.service_area: Serving Port Wentworth, the port corridor, and surrounding North Chatham County communities.
neighborhoods.content_hook:
Port Wentworth sits at the epicenter of the Port of Savannah's massive logistics operation, where thousands of 18-wheelers daily transport containers between the port terminals, I-95, and I-16. This concentration of heavy commercial truck traffic alongside residential neighborhoods creates one of the most dangerous traffic environments in Chatham County.
Roden Law's Savannah office represents Port Wentworth residents injured in truck accidents, car crashes involving commercial vehicles, I-95 and I-16 interstate collisions, and premises liability cases. Our attorneys have extensive experience with the federal trucking regulations that govern Port of Savannah truck traffic.
Injured in Port Wentworth? Call (912) 303-5850 for a free case review.

faqs.question: Why are truck accidents so common in Port Wentworth?
faqs.answer: Port Wentworth is adjacent to the Port of Savannah, one of the busiest container ports in the nation. Thousands of 18-wheelers traverse local roads daily, mixing with residential traffic. Driver fatigue, heavy loads, and the pressure to meet shipping deadlines contribute to a high rate of truck-related accidents.

faqs.question: Can I sue a trucking company after a Port Wentworth accident?
faqs.answer: Yes. Trucking companies are liable for their drivers' negligence under Georgia's respondeat superior doctrine. Federal Motor Carrier Safety Regulations also impose strict requirements on maintenance, driver hours, and load limits. Violations can strengthen your case significantly.

faqs.question: What damages can I recover after a truck accident?
faqs.answer: Truck accidents often cause severe injuries. You may recover medical expenses, lost wages, pain and suffering, and potentially punitive damages if the trucking company's conduct was egregious. Georgia law does not cap compensatory damages in most personal injury cases.

neighborhoods.name: Georgia Southern University Area
neighborhoods.h1: Personal Injury Lawyer Serving the Georgia Southern University Area, Statesboro
neighborhoods.meta_title: Georgia Southern Area Injury Lawyer | Statesboro | Roden Law
neighborhoods.meta_description: Injured near Georgia Southern University in Statesboro? Roden Law handles car accidents, pedestrian injuries, and campus-area incidents. Free consultation.
neighborhoods.roads: US-301 (Main Street) through the Georgia Southern area carries heavy traffic mixing university commuters, commercial vehicles, and student pedestrian traffic. Chandler Road and the surrounding student housing corridors see heavy pedestrian and bicycle traffic from students walking and biking to campus. The Chandler Road and Fair Road intersection is one of Statesboro's most accident-prone junctions due to high volumes and complex turning patterns. Lanier Drive and Veterans Memorial Parkway serve as alternative university access routes but increasingly handle overflow traffic.
neighborhoods.hospitals: East Georgia Regional Medical Center — 1499 Fair Rd, Statesboro, GA 30458 — (912) 486-1000
neighborhoods.landmarks: Georgia Southern University campus, Paulson Stadium, Chandler Road student corridor, Statesboro town center, Bulloch County Courthouse
neighborhoods.service_area: Serving the Georgia Southern University area, student housing corridors, and surrounding Statesboro neighborhoods.
neighborhoods.content_hook:
The Georgia Southern University area is the heart of Statesboro, where approximately 27,000 students create a dynamic but challenging traffic environment. The mix of student pedestrians and cyclists, university commuter traffic, and commercial vehicles on roads not designed for current volumes produces frequent accidents, particularly along Chandler Road and the US-301 corridor.
Roden Law's Savannah office represents Georgia Southern area residents and students injured in car accidents, pedestrian and bicycle collisions near campus, and premises liability incidents at off-campus housing and businesses. Our attorneys practice in Bulloch County Superior Court.
Injured near Georgia Southern? Call (912) 303-5850 for a free consultation.

faqs.question: Does Roden Law handle cases in Bulloch County?
faqs.answer: Yes. Our Savannah office handles personal injury cases in Bulloch County Superior Court, serving Statesboro, the Georgia Southern University area, and surrounding communities.

faqs.question: Can a Georgia Southern student file an injury claim?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. Students have the same legal rights as any other injured person. Whether you're injured in a car accident, bicycle crash, or premises liability incident near campus, you can pursue compensation. Georgia's 2-year statute of limitations applies.

faqs.question: What if I'm hit by a car while biking to campus?
faqs.answer: Cyclists have the same rights as motorists on Georgia roads. If a driver's negligence caused your bicycle accident, you can pursue full compensation for medical expenses, lost wages, pain and suffering, and other damages. Document the scene and seek medical attention immediately.
